title: paspalum campinarum
intro: Paspalum campinarum, also known as paspalum, is a type of tropical grass native to Central and South America, classified as a type of warm-season grass. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum campinarum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Paspalum campinarum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments.
